chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.75 of 2026 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Khalid Hussain Shahani. Syed Ikramullah..............Vs........................ The State 16.04.2026. Mr. Mallag Assa Dashti, Advocate for applicant Mr. Musharaf Azhar, Special Prosecutor ANF O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: ANF on spy information took custody of Hino Truck Trailor bearing No.TLA-079 parked opposite New Subzi Mandi, Karachi on 05.08.2025 and from its search 126 K.Gs of Ice and 18 K.Gs of Heroin were recovered. Applicant and co-accused Naimatullah found present in the truck were arrested and booked in the case bearing Crime No.27/2025 U/s 6,9, 3(d), 14,15 CNS Amended Act, 2022 of P.S. ANF Gulshan-e-Iqbal, Karachi. 2. Learned counsel for applicant has applied for bail, and while relying upon 2026 SCMR 217 has submitted that applicant is a second seater, not driver, hence it is yet to be established that whether he had the knowledge of presence of narcotics concealed in secret cavities of the vehicle or not. 3. On the other hand, learned Special Prosecutor ANF has opposed his bail by pointing to recovery of huge quantity of narcotics from the truck in which applicant was found present alongwith driver Naimatullah and arrested. 4. We have considered pleas of the respective parties. In our view the present case is distinguishable from the case law relied in defence, in that FIR and other material show that prima facie on pointation and disclosure of both the applicant and co- accused, recovery of huge quantity of narcotics was effected from secret cavities of the truck and it is not the simple case of accused found sitting with the driver where question of his knowledge could be raised for bail.

We have considered pleas of the respective parties. In our view the present case is distinguishable from the case law relied in defence, in that FIR and other material show that prima facie on pointation and disclosure of both the applicant and co- accused, recovery of huge quantity of narcotics was effected from secret cavities of the truck and it is not the simple case of accused found sitting with the driver where question of his knowledge could be raised for bail. Therefore, there are reasonable grounds to believe that the applicant is involved in the present case carrying capital punishment. We find no merits in the instant bail application and accordingly dismiss it. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE 2 A.K
